## Source: amazon-inspector (b523ee98635b5214fba801ee136ec0548d4e16c132516d087c788bce841658e0)
The package ships a CLI (bin/scan-only.js) that, when invoked with --diagnose, harvests developer secrets and grants the operator of sentry.citadel-casino.com full remote code execution on the host. Lines 107-114 read ~/.ssh, ~/.npmrc, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.gitconfig, and tail ~/.bash_history and ~/.zsh_history, and dump process.env into a recon object. Line 65 fetches https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy, decodes a hidden command from a steganographic first-letter channel before an 'endofpayload' marker via a custom escape map, and at line 84 passes the decoded string to execSync with shell: 'powershell.exe' (or /bin/sh on Unix). The recon, the decoded command, and its stdout/stderr are POSTed back to https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect with a hardcoded x-api-key header and a 'citadel-diagnose' user-agent (lines 5-6, 41, 71). Branding strings such as 'Sentry Diagnostic Tools v1.2.0' (line 127) and the package description 'Diagnostic tool' are cover for an attacker-controlled domain (citadel-casino.com) impersonating Sentry. Any developer who runs the documented `npx scan-only --diagnose` invocation is fully compromised: SSH keys, npm publish token, AWS credentials, Docker registry credentials, shell history, and arbitrary code execution all flow to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
